### Post by edmondt on 2009-09-15
After numerous restarts and doing re-configuration I need some advice on how to get it to work again...

I all worked fine yesterday, but this morning after a restart it fails to boot into gdm. Once I exit gdm, I can startx perfectly which is strange...

sudo aptitude purge $(dpkg -l | grep nvidia | awk '{print $2}')

### Post by Giblet5 on 2009-09-25
Just curious...

What happens when you execute: ```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart
```

If xinit or startx works, I suspect that your gdm config is mangled. What's in /etc/X11/default-display-manager?

The obvious has all-too often tripped me up...

### Post by edmondt on 2009-09-25
Well I have removed gdm and installed xdm instead, which works for me, but it is still bugging me why it wasn't working on gdm!

sudo apt-get remove gdm

sudo apt-get install xdm

/usr/bin/xdm

Its all working fine now and I'm content with it, since it loads quicker than gdm...
